project: Recipe Club Hub - Bug Fixes & Polish
description: Fix recipe image upload URLs, grocery re-combining, pantry standardization, club recipe deletion guards, and migrate Google Calendar calls to an edge function with refresh token support.

Tests: tests/unit/components/mealplan/MealPlanPage.test.tsx — remove any assertion that checks for 'Added ... to plan' toast text.

userStories.notes:
File: src/components/recipes/GroceryListSection.tsx. Line 377: `{excludedItems.map((item) => (
  • {item.name}
    • ))}` — change to display `item.name` with first letter capitalized. Use a simple inline transform: `{item.name.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + item.name.slice(1)}`. Tests: tests/unit/components/recipes/GroceryListSection.test.tsx — verify the exclusion list renders 'Salt' not 'salt'.

userStories.notes: File: src/components/recipes/RecipeCard.tsx, lines 255-276. The ingredients display currently shows ALL ingredients with no pantry filtering. Import DEFAULT_PANTRY_ITEMS from @/lib/pantry (already exported after US-007 from previous PRD). Filter ingredients before display: `const filteredIngredients = ingredients?.filter(ing => !DEFAULT_PANTRY_ITEMS.includes(ing.name.toLowerCase()));`. Use filteredIngredients for both the count display ('N ingredients') and the expanded list. Also update the CATEGORY_ORDER filter to use filteredIngredients. Tests: Add a test that passes ingredients including 'salt' and 'pepper' and verifies they don't appear in the rendered output.

userStories.title: Block deletion of club event recipes from Recipes tab
userStories.description: As a user, I should not be able to delete recipes linked to club events from the Recipes tab. Club event recipes should only be removed from within the event itself.
acceptanceCriteria: Recipes linked to club events (recipe.eventId is not null) do NOT show a delete button in RecipeHub
Personal recipes still show the delete button
Club recipes NOT linked to events can still be deleted (if any exist)
The edit button remains gated behind recipe.isPersonal
RecipeCard.tsx and RecipeHub.tsx tests updated
RecipeCard.tsx and RecipeHub.tsx show 100% coverage
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
Files: src/components/recipes/RecipeHub.tsx, src/components/recipes/RecipeCard.tsx. This partially reverts US-010 from the previous PRD. Two approaches:

**Option A (recommended):** In RecipeHub.tsx, when passing onDelete to RecipeCard, filter by eventId: `onDelete={recipe.eventId ? undefined : handleDeleteRecipe}`. This way RecipeCard doesn't need to change — it just won't receive onDelete for event-linked recipes.

**Option B:** Add an eventId guard back to handleDeleteRecipe (line ~473-490): `const recipe = [...clubRecipes, ...personalRecipes].find(r => r.id === recipeId); if (recipe?.eventId) { setDeleteGuardMessage('Remove this recipe from within the event page.'); return; }`.

Option A is cleaner — the delete button simply doesn't render. In RecipeHub, the RecipeCard mapping (around line 740-750) currently passes `onDelete={handleDeleteRecipe}` for all recipes. Change to check `recipe.eventId`. Tests: Verify club event recipes don't show delete button, personal recipes still do.

acceptanceCriteria: parse-recipe edge function detects localhost/127.0.0.1 storage URLs and rewrites them using SUPABASE_URL env var
Uploaded images from local dev are fetchable by the edge function
Production URLs continue to work unchanged
The fix handles both http://127.0.0.1:54321 and http://localhost:54321 patterns
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Grocery combining — only re-combine when recipes change, not on tab visit
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Groceries tab in Meal Plan to load cached combined ingredients instantly, only re-combining when I add, remove, or edit a recipe for that week.
acceptanceCriteria: Visiting the Groceries tab in Meal Plan loads from cache without calling the combine-ingredients edge function
Adding a recipe to the meal plan triggers combining (and saves to cache)
Removing a recipe from the meal plan triggers combining (and saves to cache)
Editing a recipe (URL change + re-parse) triggers combining
The combine-ingredients edge function is NOT called when simply switching to the Groceries tab if cache is fresh
MealPlanPage.test.tsx updated to verify caching behavior
MealPlanPage.tsx shows 100% coverage
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
File: src/components/mealplan/MealPlanPage.tsx. Root cause: The useEffect at lines 297-324 has `loadGroceryData` as a dependency (line 324). This function reference changes on every render because its own dependency array (line 244) includes `items` state. Events avoid this by depending on `recipesWithNotes.length` — a stable number.

**Fix approach:**
1. Stabilize the useEffect dependencies. Replace `loadGroceryData` in the dependency array with a stable trigger. Options:
   - Use a `groceryCacheKey` ref or state that only changes when recipes are added/removed/edited
   - OR memoize `loadGroceryData` more aggressively with `useCallback` that doesn't depend on `items`
   - OR use a separate `groceryDirty` flag that's set to true when recipes change and false after combining

2. **Recommended approach:** Add a `groceryDirty` boolean state (default true). Set it to true in: handleAddCustomMeal success, handleAddRecipeMeal success, parse completion, recipe deletion. The Groceries tab useEffect checks: if groceryDirty, load + combine + set dirty=false. If not dirty, just load from cache. Change the useEffect dependency to `[viewTab, groceryDirty, weekStart, userId]` — all stable values.

3. On initial page load, groceryDirty starts true so the first Groceries tab visit loads and combines if needed. After that, switching tabs doesn't re-trigger.

4. Keep the existing cache check (lines 305-317) as a second layer of protection — even if groceryDirty is true, the cache check prevents unnecessary AI calls if recipe IDs haven't changed.

Tests: Verify that switching to Groceries tab multiple times doesn't call combine-ingredients repeatedly. Verify that adding a recipe then visiting Groceries does trigger combining.

userStories.title: Migration — create user_tokens table for Google OAuth refresh tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database table to store Google OAuth refresh tokens so the Google Calendar edge function can mint fresh access tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: A new migration creates the user_tokens table with: user_id (FK to auth.users), provider, refresh_token, updated_at
RLS policy: users can only read/write their own row
UNIQUE constraint on (user_id, provider)
Service role has full access (for edge function use)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
Create a new migration file in supabase/migrations/. Follow the naming pattern of existing migrations (e.g., 20260223000001_create_user_tokens.sql). Schema:
```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.user_tokens (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  provider TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'google',
  refresh_token TEXT NOT NULL,
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  UNIQUE (user_id, provider)
);

ALTER TABLE public.user_tokens ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

CREATE POLICY "Users can manage their own tokens" ON public.user_tokens
  FOR ALL USING (auth.uid() = user_id)
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
```
This is a migration-only story — no TypeScript changes.

userStories.title: Auth flow — request offline access and store Google refresh token
userStories.description: As a user, when I sign in with Google, I want my refresh token stored so Google Calendar calls work even after the access token expires.
acceptanceCriteria: signInWithGoogle adds queryParams: { access_type: 'offline', prompt: 'consent' } to the OAuth options
An onAuthStateChange listener captures provider_refresh_token on SIGNED_IN events
The refresh token is upserted into the user_tokens table
If no refresh token is present (e.g., email login), no upsert is attempted
Existing auth flows (email login, token refresh) still work correctly
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Edge function — google-calendar for create/update/delete with token refresh
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a google-calendar edge function that handles all Calendar API calls server-side, using stored refresh tokens to mint fresh access tokens.
acceptanceCriteria: New edge function 'google-calendar' accepts { action, calendarEventId?, date, time, ingredientName, attendeeEmails? }
Function reads user's refresh_token from user_tokens table using the JWT user ID
Function exchanges refresh_token for a fresh access_token via Google's token endpoint
Supports action: 'create' — creates calendar event with attendees, Google Meet, reminders
Supports action: 'update' — updates event date/time/title
Supports action: 'delete' — deletes calendar event
Returns { success, eventId?, error? }
Uses GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET from env (must be configured as Supabase secrets)
Gracefully handles missing refresh token with clear error message
verify_jwt is true (requires authenticated user)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update googleCalendar.ts to call edge function instead of direct API

acceptanceCriteria: createCalendarEvent calls supabase.functions.invoke('google-calendar', { body: { action: 'create', ... } })
updateCalendarEvent calls supabase.functions.invoke('google-calendar', { body: { action: 'update', ... } })
deleteCalendarEvent calls supabase.functions.invoke('google-calendar', { body: { action: 'delete', ... } })
provider_token is no longer accessed from the session in these functions
getClubMemberEmails is called client-side and passed to the edge function for 'create' action
Dev mode checks (isDevMode()) are preserved at the top of each function
Return types remain the same: { success, eventId?, error? }
All callers (EventDetailPage, etc.) continue to work without changes
googleCalendar.test.ts updated if it exists
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.activeForm: Updating googleCalendar.ts to use edge function

userStories.title: PersonalMealDetailPage — rename 'Mark as Cooked' to 'Rate Recipes' and trigger rating flow
userStories.description: As a user, when I click 'Rate Recipes' on a meal, I want it to open the rating dialog first, and on completion automatically mark the meal as cooked. The separate 'Rate Recipes' option in the hamburger menu should be removed.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Mark as Cooked' button is renamed to 'Rate Recipes'
Clicking 'Rate Recipes' opens the EventRatingDialog for all recipes in this meal
On rating completion (onComplete callback), the meal is automatically marked as cooked (cooked_at set on meal_plan_items)
After the flow completes, the UI shows the green 'Cooked' badge with 'Undo' button
The 'Rate Recipes' option is removed from the hamburger dropdown menu
If the meal has no recipes, the button is hidden (same as current 'Mark as Cooked' behavior)
The 'Undo' cook flow remains unchanged
Toast on success: 'Recipes rated and meal marked as cooked!'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
File: src/pages/PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx.

**Current state (from previous PRD US-005):**
- 'Mark as Cooked' button in the Event Info card (after line ~662)
- handleMarkCooked: updates meal_plan_items.cooked_at
- 'Rate Recipes' in hamburger dropdown (line ~624-627): opens EventRatingDialog
- ratingEvent/ratingRecipes/ratingMode state for the dialog

**Changes:**
1. Rename the button from 'Mark as Cooked' to 'Rate Recipes' (keep the Check icon or switch to Star)
2. Change the button's onClick from handleMarkCooked to open the rating dialog instead:
   - Set ratingRecipes to all recipes in this meal
   - Set ratingEvent to eventData
   - Set ratingMode to 'rating'
   - Open the dialog
3. In the EventRatingDialog onComplete callback: call handleMarkCooked (the existing function that sets cooked_at), then close the dialog, then show success toast
4. Remove 'Rate Recipes' from the hamburger menu DropdownMenuItem (line ~624-627)
5. Keep the 'Undo' button and uncook flow unchanged

**Flow:** User clicks 'Rate Recipes' → EventRatingDialog opens → user rates each recipe → clicks submit → onComplete fires → cooked_at is set → dialog closes → green 'Cooked' badge appears

Note: PersonalMealDetailPage is in src/pages/ (not in required 100% coverage directories).

userStories.title: Final verification — coverage, build, lint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that all required files maintain 100% test coverage, the build compiles cleanly, and lint passes.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run test:coverage shows 100% Stmts, Branch, Funcs, Lines on all files in src/components/events/, src/components/ingredients/, src/components/mealplan/, src/components/recipes/, src/lib/
IngredientWheel.tsx is the only exception (~55% acceptable)
npm run build passes with no TypeScript errors
npm run lint passes
No orphaned imports or unused variables
userStories.notes: Run npm run test:coverage and check every row in the coverage report. All files in the required directories must show 100%. If any file has gaps, read the source lines, understand what branch is missed, and add targeted tests. Then run npm run build and npm run lint. Key files to watch: MealPlanPage.tsx, RecipeCard.tsx, RecipeHub.tsx, GroceryListSection.tsx, googleCalendar.ts. Pages (EventDetailPage, PersonalMealDetailPage, Dashboard) are NOT in required coverage directories but existing tests must pass.
